codeReview.summary: Fail. This re-run does not materially address the prior blocking findings for VAL-CROSS-001/002/009. The handoff commit points to an unrelated backfill test change and provides no implementation SHA traceability for this switcher feature. In the current SwitcherCrossFlowTests, rendered evidence remains non-concrete, switch synchronization is still store-mutation-driven, and launch-chain proof remains hypothetical rather than exercised through launch paths.

issues.description: Handoff commitId resolves to an unrelated backfill portability commit (`AgentLensTests/Active/BackfillSchedulerTests.swift`) and does not contain switcher rendered-indicator/UI-action proof changes, so this fix feature lacks auditable implementation evidence.

Rendered evidence for VAL-CROSS-001/002 remains non-concrete: assertions only confirm the view can be inspected/rendered (`XCTAssertNotNil`) rather than asserting specific rendered active labels/indicators/accessibility text. Same pattern at line 2475.

Cross-surface switching is still driven by direct store mutation (`setActiveProfile`) while comments describe it as simulated UI action. This misses required UI-control-driven switching proof. Same pattern at lines 2592, 2773, and 2839.

VAL-CROSS-009 launch-chain proof remains hypothetical: tests state launch actions 'would use' active profiles but do not execute/verify launch integration seams from UI action paths. Same pattern at line 2858.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Skill procedure compliance is overstated: `followedProcedure=true` is set despite no context-read command evidence and no implementation-commit traceability when the referenced commit is non-feature/non-implementation for this task.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Handoff `verification.commandsRun` includes only xcodebuild runs; `.factory/skills/switcher-ui-worker/SKILL.md` requires context-read evidence and implementation SHA traceability when final commit is artifact/non-implementation.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Validation hardening expectations for rendered/action-chain proofs remain inconsistently applied: tests continue to rely on inspectability and store-state assertions instead of concrete rendered indicators and UI-triggered action paths.

summary: Review result: fail. The fix does not provide new auditable implementation evidence for this feature and does not resolve prior blockers around concrete rendered assertions, UI-action-driven switching, and launch-chain invocation proof.
